generated_oml:
// Sensing Components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor array capturing raw 3D point clouds from the vehicle's surroundings"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARDataTransmission
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing vehicle state telemetry such as speed, acceleration, and heading"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateDataTransmission
]

instance MotorOutputSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor monitoring motor output signals for the drive system"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorDataTransmission
]

instance SteeringOutputSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor recording steering actuator output to determine wheel angle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringDataTransmission
]

instance ServoOutputSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor logging servo actuator outputs for auxiliary functions"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ServoDataTransmission
]

// Physical-to-virtual Interaction: Data Transmitted
instance LiDARDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance VehicleStateDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance MotorDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorOutputSensor
]

instance SteeringDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringOutputSensor
]

instance ServoDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ServoOutputSensor
]

// Data after translation / processing
instance LiDARSerializedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Serialized and unit-converted LiDAR point cloud data conforming to ISO 23247"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LiDARClusterEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARDataTransmission
]

instance VehicleStateSerializedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Serialized vehicle state telemetry (speed, acceleration, heading) per ISO 23247"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCLogicEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateDataTransmission
]

instance MotorSerializedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Serialized motor output signals after unit conversion"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorDataTransmission
]

instance SteeringSerializedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Serialized steering actuator outputs after unit conversion"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringDataTransmission
]

instance ServoSerializedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Serialized servo actuator outputs after unit conversion"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData ServoDataTransmission
]

// Enablers
instance LiDARClusterEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "PointCloud Clustering & Tracking SW component that aggregates 2‑D LiDAR points into coherent object tracks"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ACCLogicEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ACC logic component that performs computation offloading in the cloud to determine desired speed"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DataPreProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data Pre‑Processing FE that routes serialized motor, steering, and servo data to downstream components"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance RemoteCommandEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Remote command component that processes user UI commands and translates them into vehicle ECU actions"
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

// Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Streams real‑time vehicle state, projected driving paths, and detected object tracks; allows viewing, saving, and report generation"
    DTDFVocab:provides LiDARTracksInsight, VehicleStateInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Runs ACC logic in the cloud, receives vehicle telemetry, computes desired speed, and sends command back to the vehicle ECU"
    DTDFVocab:provides SetDesiredSpeedAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables operators to enable/disable ACC, set desired speed, or issue an emergency braking command via the UI"
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCEnableAction, ACCDisableAction, EmergencyBrakeAction
]

// Insights and Actions
instance LiDARTracksInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Provides detected object tracks derived from LiDAR clustering"
]

instance VehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Provides vehicle state information such as speed, acceleration, and heading"
]

instance ACCEnableAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enables the Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) subsystem on the vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ACCDisableAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Disables the Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) subsystem on the vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Sets the desired speed for the ACC subsystem based on lead‑vehicle distance"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Triggers an emergency braking command through the vehicle ECU"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twin infrastructure mandates a shared notion of time across all devices, with a required clock synchronization precision of 100 ms, as mandated by safety‑critical vehicular standards. No explicit higher‑frequency synchronization method is discussed, but the 100 ms bound effectively sets a lower bound on the frequency of synchronized events and controls the temporal granularity of digital‑physical interactions. Latency between physical and digital operations must therefore be kept well below 100 ms to maintain safety‑critical determinism."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The orchestration follows the ISO 23247 reference architecture, mapping each functional element (FE) onto a dedicated ROS 2 package that encapsulates one or more ROS 2 nodes. Functional elements are grouped by entity: the User Entity hosts the User‑Interface FE (split into visualisation and control‑panel sub‑components), the Digital Twin Entity hosts data‑management and control‑mechanism FEs, and the Physical Twin Entity hosts data‑pre‑processing, actuation, and device‑communication FEs. The physical resources (compute, sensors, actuators) are represented by the Physical Twin Entity, which also contains automotive‑specific functional blocks such as point‑cloud clustering and tracking. This spread across separate nodes allows fine‑grained resource management, while the ISO‑23247 entity hierarchy provides system‑wide coordination of data flow, command execution, and state synchronization."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Inter‑entity communication in the DTS occurs over a Wi‑Fi network using UDP as the transport protocol, linking the vehicle’s physical twin to the digital twin processing unit. Inside the digital twin, software components communicate via a ROS 2 network which typically employs the Data Distribution Service (DDS) over multicast or shared memory. The overall network topology is star‑shaped with the vehicle controller at the center, broadcasting sensor streams and telemetry to the processing nodes. Specific bandwidth or latency requirements are not quantified in the document, but the design anticipates sufficient Wi‑Fi throughput to carry high‑rate LiDAR point‑cloud streams and vehicle telemetry. Reliability mechanisms such as redundancy or failover are not detailed, and no explicit security protocols (TLS/DTLS) are mentioned for the wireless link."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DTS is deployed as an edge‑based embedded system on the vehicle, with all real‑time processing (Data Translation FE, Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking, Vehicle State FE, etc.) executed locally on an on‑board multiprocessor platform, optionally leveraging GPU acceleration for point‑cloud processing. Data archives are stored locally, and the user interface (3D Visualization FE) communicates with the processing unit over the local Wi‑Fi network. No cloud or on‑premise data centers are referenced, and the documentation does not describe scalability mechanisms, fault‑tolerance, or availability guarantees beyond the local deployment."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system aligns with ISO 23247‑2021, specifically Part 2 Reference Architecture, which prescribes an entity‑based model comprising Observable Manufacturing Element (OME), Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity. Functional Entities within these entities may employ OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA) for device integration and data exchange, ensuring interoperability across manufacturing systems. No additional third‑party certification or compliance frameworks are specified in the document."
]
